title: AbbVie Faces Setback as USFDA Declines Approval of Wrinkle Drug TrenibotE

contentHtml:
New Delhi: AbbVie said on Thursday ‚Äãthe U.S. Food and Drug Administration (USFDA) has ‚Äådeclined to approve its experimental wrinkle treatment, trenibotE, citing issues in manufacturing.

Here are some details:

  • TrenibotE is ‚Äãa botulinum toxin for treating facial ‚Äãwrinkles such as frown lines, with effects ‚ÅÝwearing off as early as 8 hours ‚Äãcompared to Botox's duration of over two ‚Äãweeks.
  • The setback removes a near-term catalyst for AbbVie‚Äôs aesthetics business, but Street expectations for trenibotE were modest, Citi ‚Äãanalyst Geoff Meacham said.
  • Meacham also noted ‚Äãthat manufacturing‚Äërelated requests have likely delayed approval into 2027.
  • AbbVie ‚Äåadded ‚ÅÝthe agency did not raise concerns about the treatment's safety or efficacy, and did not ask for new patient studies.
  • The company dominates ‚Äãthe aesthetics ‚Äãmarket, generating ‚ÅÝmulti-billion-dollar revenues from its Botox treatment, which are used for wrinkle ‚Äãsmoothing, migraines and muscle disorders.
  • The company ‚Äãsaid ‚ÅÝtrenibotE has been studied in more than 2,100 patients, including two late-stage trials and a ‚ÅÝsafety ‚Äãstudy.
  • AbbVie said regulatory reviews of ‚Äãthe treatment in other countries are still underway.

Source : Reuters
